mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides best-practice 'fast paths' for bootstrapping a secure and minimal viable cluster. While it is user-friendly, it requires a good understanding of Kubernetes concepts for effective use.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for bootstrapping the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

```

> Add the Google Cloud public key for package verification.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list to include the Kubernetes packages.
> Install specific versions of kubeadm, kubelet, and kubectl.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This command bootstraps the Kubernetes control plane on the master node.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version=v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig for the Admin User
steps.description:
This step configures kubectl to use the credentials of the admin user to interact with the cluster.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create a directory for kubeconfig.
> Copy the admin config file to the user's kube directory.
> Change ownership of the kube config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Install Calico as the network add-on.

steps.title: Verify the Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will revert any changes made by kubeadm and remove the cluster configuration. Data loss may occur.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf /etc/kubernetes

```

> Reset the kubeadm configuration.
> Remove Kubernetes configuration files.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup the current cluster state, upgrade kubeadm, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current node states.
> Upgrade kubeadm, kubelet, and kubectl to the new version.
> Check available upgrades.
> Apply the upgrade to the cluster.
> Verify that all nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.
commands: sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubelet service may not be started or is failing to run.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Possible network issues or insufficient resources on the node.

**Fix:**
Ensure network connectivity and that the node meets the resource requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x:6443'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check if the API server is running and accessible on the specified IP and port.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CNI plugin not installed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** A network add-on has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Install a CNI plugin such as Calico or Flannel.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features in Kubernetes 1.31, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain flags related to network configuration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new scheduling policies.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in a 'Ready' state.
codeSnippets: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
